feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud, connectivity
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud, connectivity Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: unable to upload excel file into SAC trail version
entry.content:
Hi,

can anyone please help me.

I'm a beginner of SAC. while I'm trying to upload the Excel file into SAC Trail version instead of creating the acquire data it is being downing as a dataset.

have attached the screenshot.

entry.title: Preparation Cerification SAC
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I’m currently preparing for the C_SAC_2501 exam, and I have some doubts about a few questions. Please, I would really appreciate your help to better understand the correct answer to each question.


You have a dimension with members for product groups and products. Each product group has associated products. You want to plan by product group without disaggregating into the products.

How can you do this?

Disable allocations
Use two properties
Disable distribution
Use two hierarchies


You are creating a new public version. Which categories can you use?

Note: There are 2 correct answers to this question.

Forecast
Budget
Predictive
Actual
You are creating a script for an advanced data action. Which character designates a virtual variable member?

%
/
#
.


You have a story in My Files. You want your colleague to review and comment on the story. What must you do?

Create a Review task for the story
Add a Comment widget to the story
Include the story in a Discussion
Share with View access


What can you do to reduce the data refresh time when opening a story? Note: There are 3 correct answers to this question.

Avoid tables with more than 500 rows and 60 columns
Load widgets in the background
Avoid prompts
Use responsive pages
Use conditional formatting


You have a live data model with two dimensions: Firstname and Lastname. Users want a single dimension in the data model that displays the dimensions as Lastname, Firstname.

What must you do?

Create the combined data in the source system data model.
Create a calculated dimension in the story.
Create a dimension in the live data model.
Group the Firstname and Lastname in the data model.

 


You have a story based on an import model. The transaction data in the model's data source changes.

How can you update the data in the model?

Note: There are 2 correct answers to this question.

Refresh the import job
Schedule the import
Allow model import
Refresh the story


You have a dataset that extracts data from an SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW) system. The data in the SAP BW system changes. How can you update the dataset?

You must refresh the story that uses the dataset.
You must manually reimport the data.
You can schedule the dataset to update on a regular basis.
You must create a new dataset.


Which SAP Analytics Cloud feature uses natural language processing?

Smart insight
Data analyzer
Digital boardroom
Just Ask feature


Which automatically created dimension type can you delete from an analytic data model?

Date
Generic
Version
Organization

entry.title: Transporting Deleted Teams from Test to Production in SAP SAC
entry.content:
Dear Team,

As part of our recent housekeeping activity, we identified several unused Teams in  SAP Analytics Cloud  and have deleted them in the Test environment. To maintain consistency across systems, I would like to transport these deletions to the Production environment.

Could anyone please guide me through the steps required to perform this transport? Specifically, I want to ensure that all Teams deleted in Test are also removed in Production following the transport process.

Thank you for your support.

Regards,
Om Pandya

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud: Creating Age From a DOB
entry.content: How can I get Age from a DOB At Model Level by using Toggle Custom Editor. I Have empty column with Age

entry.title: sap sac model
entry.content: My source system is SuccessFactors where Date of Birth is maintained in it. I want to create a model  that calculate age using Date of Birth field. Is it possible to create calculation in SAC to calculate age using the DOB column?

entry.title: SAC Certification
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I’m currently preparing for the C_SAC_2501 exam, and I have some doubts about a few questions. Please, I would really appreciate your help to better understand the correct answer to each question.

 

How can you improve the performance of advanced data actions? Note:

There are 3 correct answers to this question.

Use fewer MEMBERSET statements
Use fewer aggregation dimension functions
Use fewer data functions
Use fewer FOREACH functions
Use fewer IF statements

-------------------------

What can you do with a multi action? 

Note: There are 2 correct answers to this question. 

  • Run allocation processes 
  • Approve data 
  • Run allocation data actions 
  • Import transaction data 

---------------------------------------

 

You are creating a data action to copy data from one year to the next. 

In the parameter for the source year, which default setting must you change? 

  • Granularity 
  • Level 
  • Hierarchy 
  • Cardinality 

 

 

------------------------------------

When scrolling down in a long table, how can you retain column headers? Note: There are 2 correct answers to this question. 

  • Enable Keeping member names visible 
  • Freeze Dimension Header Column 
  • Enable Auto-Size and Page Table Vertically 
  • Freeze Dimension Header Row 

 

 

-----------------------------------------------------

 

What can you do to reduce the data refresh time when opening a story? Note: There are 3 correct answers to this question. 

  • Avoid tables with more than 500 rows and 60 columns 
  • Load widgets in the background 
  • Avoid prompts 
  • Use responsive pages 
  • Use conditional formatting 

 

---------------------------------------------------

What is the main benefit a business user can gain from using SAP Business Data Cloud:  

  • Data Management 
  • Data Analysis 
  • Data Integration 
  • Data Intelligence 

 

Thanks!!

entry.title: Error importing data
entry.content:
Hi team, 

So I have a problem. I am an Admin user in SAC, so I can import the data (from Datasphere) without any issues. The problem occurs when I switch to the end-user account, which has the Planner Reporter role, Viewer role, and BI Content Viewer role. In this account, I can’t import the data in a Multi-Action that contains a Data Import step. The error was:

Do you know the issue?

Thanks in Advance,
Ganjos

entry.title: SAC SSO: Merging existing users without losing content
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

we have IAS configured as a proxy. Users are provisioned from a custom IdP to IAS and then created dynamically in SAC (attribute = User ID) via SSO .

In SAC, there are already manually created users with existing content.
The SSO users and the manually created users in SAC have different User IDs, which is historical.

Our goal is to switch fully to SSO and replace or merge the existing SAC users without losing user content (stories, private objects, settings).

Currently, we face the following issues:

  • SAC does not allow duplicate email addresses when a new SSO user is created (different User ID, but same email address).

  • When we assign roles manually in SAC to an SSO user, they are removed at the next login.

Our questions:

  • What is the recommended approach to dynamically create SSO users in SAC and transfer or replace existing content from the old user to the new SSO user?

  • Is there a simple or supported way to migrate or merge existing SAC users with the new SSO users?

  • How are Owner property handled when SSO is activated, and what should be considered to avoid issues with access or content ownership?

Thank you very much for your help and experience.

Best regards

entry.title: Running total for a set of dimension in SAC Story on S/4 Hana Live Connection
entry.content:
MaterialWerkDateOrder NumberOrder QuantityTotal Quantity
32222AT4301.12.202522224040
32222AT4302.12.20252223242
32222AT4304.12.2025400050142
32222AT4304.12.2025500050142
32222AT4501.12.2025700011
32222AT4305.12.202550001143
32222AT4502.12.2025700023

I want to show running total for a combination of Material and WERK  in the column Total Quantity based on Order Quantity when ordered by Date in ascending Order. In the below example for 

1st record : 40 for total quantity

2nd record : 40+2 = 42 for total quantity

3rd record and 4th record  , there are 2 orders for same day and same combination , so the total Quantity will become 42 + 50 + 50 = 142 and will be shown in both rows of 3rd and 4th 

5th record , there is a different WERK , so it will have 1 for Total Quantity 

6th record , again same material and WERK combination , so the Order quantity will get added to 3rd/4th row value and show as 143 for Total Quantity

7th record again has same combination of Material and WERK , so the order Quantity will get added to Total Quantity value of 5th record and show as 3 as Total Quantity in 7th record.

entry.title: create a new connection to external content repositories from SAC
entry.content: Hi SAP Community, I am currently looking for any KBA related regarding How to create a new connection to external content repositories from SAC it will be helpful for us to have a basic view. Thanks

entry.title: Consuming SAC export API into Excel ODATA feed
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We are currently exploring options to export a Public Dimension from SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) using the Export API.

We have already successfully tested the Export API using Postman, and the API is returning the expected response. However, the key challenge we are facing is how to consume this data in Excel format.

When we attempt to create a connection in Excel using an OData feed, we are redirected only to the SAC login page. Excel does not provide an option to enter credentials explicitly, and as a result, we are unable to authenticate and complete the connection. This leaves us unclear on how to import the SAC dimension data into Excel through OData.

From a business perspective, our client was previously using SAP BPC for Planning, where exporting dimensions (including hierarchies) was straightforward and user-friendly. In SAC, however, there does not appear to be an equivalent out-of-the-box functionality to export large public dimensions directly to Excel.

Additionally, manual copy-paste is not a viable option, as the dimension contains a very large number of members, and the hierarchy structure also needs to be preserved.

Given these constraints, we would appreciate guidance on:

How to export Public Dimensions from SAC into Excel format

How to handle authentication when using OData or Export APIs with Excel

Best practices or recommended approaches to export large dimensions with hierarchy

Any suggestions, alternative approaches, or SAP-recommended solutions would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Sap Analytic cloud export data from Model to SFTP
entry.content:
Hi experts,
I want create a schedule to export data from model in sap analytic cloud to SFTP server.
Can you help me to create configuration?
At now, when I add new connection and choose SFTP, system show this message:

Thanks

entry.title: Is it possible to restrict users from using S4HANA live connection in SAC?
entry.content:
Is it possible to restrict users from using S4HANA live connection in SAC? The users should still be able to run the stories or Insights that were created on S4 HANA Live connection.
I tried to restrict by unchecking below, but it did not work. Despite unchecking it I could execute the report that was built on S4 Live connection. So, this authorization right appears to be not working.


I came across an SAP note (3200367 - Restrict Connections for users in SAC) that says it is not possible and there's an idea registered on "Influence" portal regarding restricting users from using live connections. This idea is set for long term consideration.
Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence
The note is old, wanted to check if there's any recommendation? how others in the community manage this?

Thank you!
Parag

entry.title: SAC Story fails to load on second open – “Communication with the remote system failed”
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m facing an issue in SAP Analytics Cloud.
The story loads normally the first time, but when I close it and reopen it in the same SAC session, I get this error:

“Communication with the remote system failed.”

The story uses a Live Connection. After logging out and in again, the first load works fine, but the second always fails.

Has anyone seen this behavior or knows what might cause it?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAC Story fails to load on second open – “Communication with the remote system failed”
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m facing an issue in SAP Analytics Cloud.
The story loads normally the first time, but when I close it and reopen it in the same SAC session, I get this error:

“Communication with the remote system failed.”

The story uses a Live Connection. 

Has anyone seen this behavior or knows what might cause it?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAC: Story fails to connect when opening from URL
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

A client can open and consume a story normally when he opens the story from the folder where it is located.

When he opens the story using a URL link, an error pops up: Cannot connect to BW system...

He tried, with equal results, these two URLs to the story:

- the one I copied for him from the "share" option of the story (URL ending with the story's UID)

- the one he copied from the browser when successfully opening the story from the folder (ending with [story UID]/?mode=view)

We need direct links to the story (used to open from Fiori).

Any idea what could be the problem?

Thanks for your inputs!

Stéphane

entry.title: Story not loading for a single user of SAC
entry.content:
Hi,

I cannot work with a story in SAC as it is not loading completely. This happens only to me and no other users of our analytics team.

When I run the story, the first page does not load completely as all the visualizations sourced from a particular live connection model do not load throwing a 'failed to fetch data' error. The second page charts do not load at all. When I go to Edit mode, the following errors are thrown:

'We cannot connect to your BW System ..............'

When I try to use the builder panel on the left to modify the story, I get the error:

'Builder is currently unable to access this datasource'

I have the same access permissions as that of my team members who do not face this problem. I have similar problem with some other stories in our environment as well which the other team members do not face.

Please suggest what could be going wrong and how I can narrow down to the root cause of this problem.

entry.title: Is there a good way to automatically export a printed SAC story with different input control filter?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a story that includes three different input control filters. I need to select various values in these filters and export the story as a PDF. However, the three filters result in a total of 90 combinations, which makes manual exporting impractical. I’m wondering whether there is any good way to automatically export a printed SAC story using different input control filter values.

I’ve considered using Publications with bookmarks, but 90 combinations would require creating 90 separate publications, and I don’t have enough licenses for that. I also looked into batch export, but it appears to support only a single story filter, so it doesn’t fit my use case.

Any tips or guidance would be highly appreciated! Thank you in advance!

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: How can i export data through the model data management which located in Workspace?
entry.content:
Hello, expert!

There's a way to export model data through the data management which located in workspace?

or Can i export data only in public folder(in data management)?

 

Regards, HJ.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Connection To Sharepoint via Open Connectors
entry.content:
Hi,

I am wondering if anybody else has used the functionality to take flat file data from Sharepoint via Open Connectors into SAC?

We have recently, and are still experiencing a problem, in that we had a secret that was going to expire for the OAuth so I attempted to update it in the connection.

While the connection refused to save, it has now rendered the connection useless. I think this is to do with the fact that users are now being told to use Microsoft Graph, however I have turned this on in the Open connector instance but to no avail.

The Sharepoint instance in Open Connectors works fine, however it will only use the redirect from an Entra app of https://auth.cloudelements.io/oauth.

I am sure it used to work with https://bocauth.us1.sapbusinessobjects.cloud:443 but this could be to do with the Graph changes - not sure.

If I try to create a connection in SAC to Sharepoint I get no prompt for the Trust button and the connection just says there was a problem getting the authorization code.

Has anybody got experience of setting up SAC to talk to Sharepoint via Open Connectors? I am not even sure if how we are trying to do it is the correct way. There are docs on it, however they all either miss out key areas or refer to the old way of trusting the Entra app with a button which I think is being deprecated. I just need an example of a working solution and hopefully the penny will drop.

Also - is the appID and secret used in the SAC connection the same details as was used in the Open Connectors Instance? It doesn't feel right that the same details need to be applied in two different places, however this used to work but its raised questions on how our current setup is.

Any help much appreciated.
